description: Target iOS no mesmo projeto Xcode — compartilhar SwiftUI views entre macOS e iPhone

userStories.title: Add iOS target to XcodeGen project.yml
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an iOS target in the same Xcode project so both platforms share code.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'ErestorApp-iOS' target in project.yml with platform: iOS, deploymentTarget: 17.0
iOS target includes shared source files: Models/, Services/ChatService.swift, Services/ErestorConfig.swift, Views/ (all SwiftUI views)
iOS target excludes macOS-specific files: Services/BubbleWindowController.swift, Services/GlobalHotkey.swift, Views/ChatWebViewVC.swift (if still exists)
iOS target has its own Info.plist with UILaunchScreen and required capabilities
Add iOS-specific entitlements file with push notification capability
xcodegen generate succeeds and creates both targets
xcodebuild -scheme ErestorApp (macOS) still builds successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: ~/projetos/erestor/ErestorApp/project.yml. The current project has only one macOS target. XcodeGen supports multi-platform targets. Use #if os(macOS) / #if os(iOS) in shared files where platform-specific code is needed. The SwiftUI views from the panel redesign (Views/) should compile for both platforms.

userStories.title: Create iOS app entry point and main view
userStories.description: As an iPhone user, I want a fullscreen Erestor app showing the context panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ErestorApp/iOS/ErestorApp_iOS.swift with @main App struct
Main view shows ContextPanelView fullscreen (no bubble, no floating panel)
iOS app requests notification permissions on launch
iOS app registers for remote notifications (APNs) and sends device token to backend via POST /api/device/register
Use #if os(iOS) guards in shared files (ChatService, ErestorConfig) where needed
ErestorConfig points to same API URL (erestor-api.kevineger.com.br)
iOS target builds successfully (xcodebuild -scheme ErestorApp-iOS -destination 'generic/platform=iOS')
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The ContextPanelView from the panel redesign should work on both platforms. The iOS version is fullscreen — no NSPanel, no bubble. The mobile prototype in prototipo-painel.html shows the phone layout with extra agenda section. Device token registration uses the /api/device/register endpoint from the APNs backend PRD.

userStories.title: Add day timeline view for mobile
userStories.description: As an iPhone user, I want to see my full day agenda since the phone has more vertical space.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ErestorApp/Views/DayTimelineView.swift — vertical list of today's events
Each row shows: time (mono 10pt muted, 38pt min width) + event title (11pt, colored by type: green=work, dim=other)
DayTimelineView accepts: events ([ContextSummary.NextEvent]) or equivalent array
Section header 'agenda' in mono 9pt dim, uppercase, 1px letter spacing
DayTimelineView only appears on iOS (use #if os(iOS) or check horizontalSizeClass)
Add DayTimelineView to ContextPanelView between tasks and chat input on iOS
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference: the 'agenda' section in the mobile prototype (prototipo-painel.html, around line 541-566). Shows times and event names in a compact vertical list. The /api/context endpoint already returns today_events array.
